SourceDocument.title: SourceDocument
SourceDocument.description: Represents an uploaded PDF file from which educational content is extracted and processed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SourceDocument entity.

filename.description: Original filename of the uploaded PDF document.

uploadDate.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

fileSizeKB.description: Size of the uploaded document in kilobytes.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file, e.g., 'application/pdf'.

status.description: Current processing status of the document (e.g., 'uploaded', 'processing', 'processed', 'failed').

ContentChunk.title: ContentChunk
ContentChunk.description: Represents a manageable segment of text extracted and preprocessed from a SourceDocument.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentChunk entity.

sourceDocumentId.description: Reference to the SourceDocument from which this chunk was extracted. (Relationship: SourceDocument 1:N ContentChunk)

chunkIndex.description: The sequential index of this chunk within its source document.

grade.description: The target grade level for the educational content in this chunk.

subject.description: The subject category of the content (e.g., 'Math', 'Science', 'English').

topic.description: A specific topic within the subject covered by this chunk (e.g., 'Shapes', 'Plants', 'Grammar').

text.description: The cleaned and extracted text content of this chunk.

wordCount.description: The number of words in the text content.

extractionDate.description: Timestamp when the content chunk was extracted.

QuizQuestion.title: QuizQuestion
QuizQuestion.description: Represents a quiz question generated from a ContentChunk using an LLM.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizQuestion entity.

sourceChunkId.description: Reference to the ContentChunk from which this question was generated. (Relationship: ContentChunk 1:N QuizQuestion)

questionText.description: The text of the quiz question.

questionType.description: The type of the quiz question (e.g., MCQ, True/False, Fill-in-the-blank).

options.description: An array of possible answer options for Multiple Choice Questions. Will be empty or null for other question types.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer to the quiz question.

difficulty.description: The initial difficulty level assigned to the question (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

generatedDate.description: Timestamp when the question was generated.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student interacting with the quizzes. Designed to store minimal information required for tracking progress, assuming external authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

externalAuthId.description: The ID of the student from an external authentication system, linking this record to an external user account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the student record was created.

StudentAnswer.title: StudentAnswer
StudentAnswer.description: Records a student's submitted answer to a specific quiz question, used for tracking performance and adaptive difficulty.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentAnswer entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who submitted the answer. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentAnswer)

quizQuestionId.description: Reference to the QuizQuestion that was answered. (Relationship: QuizQuestion 1:N StudentAnswer)

submittedAnswer.description: The answer submitted by the student.

isCorrect.description: Indicates whether the submitted answer was correct based on the 'correctAnswer' of the quiz question.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the answer was submitted.

difficultyAtTimeOfAnswer.description: The perceived difficulty of the question at the moment the student answered it, used for adaptive logic (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

definition.description: Stores metadata for uploaded PDF documents. Documents in this collection are owned by the 'userId' specified in the path. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' field (matching 'userId') for explicit authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns and uploaded the source document. Matches request.auth.uid.

params.name: sourceDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the source document.

definition.description: Stores manageable segments of text extracted from a SourceDocument. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' (matching the parent 'userId') and 'sourceDocumentId' for authorization independence and traceability.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent source document. Matches request.auth.uid.

params.name: sourceDocumentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent source document.

params.name: contentChunkId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content chunk.

definition.description: Stores quiz questions generated from content chunks. These documents are publicly readable by students. For management, they include denormalized 'ownerId' (from the SourceDocument owner), 'sourceDocumentId', 'sourceChunkId', 'grade', and 'subject' to facilitate efficient queries and authorization independence for write operations.

params.name: quizQuestionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz question.

definition.description: Stores student profile information. The document ID ('externalAuthId') for each student directly corresponds to their Firebase Authentication UID, enforcing path-based ownership.

params.name: externalAuthId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student, which is their Firebase Authentication UID (request.auth.uid).

definition.description: Records a student's answer to a specific quiz question. Documents in this subcollection are owned by the 'externalAuthId' in the path, ensuring only the authenticated student can manage their own answers.

params.name: externalAuthId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student who submitted the answer. Matches request.auth.uid.

params.name: studentAnswerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student's answer record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Path-Based Ownership to ensure robust security rules, efficient querying, and simplified debugging. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Denormalization of Ownership:** For `ContentChunk` documents, the `ownerId` and `sourceDocumentId` from the parent `SourceDocument` are explicitly denormalized into each `ContentChunk` document. This allows security rules to evaluate ownership (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`) directly on the `ContentChunk` document itself, eliminating the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to the parent `SourceDocument`. This approach is crucial for enabling atomic operations like batch writes or transactions across related documents without breaking security validations.
2.  **Denormalization for Quiz Questions:** `QuizQuestion` documents, though publicly readable, still require an `ownerId` for management (creation, updates, deletion). This `ownerId` is denormalized from the `SourceDocument` (via `ContentChunk`). Additionally, `grade`, `subject`, and `sourceDocumentId` from the `ContentChunk` are denormalized into `QuizQuestion` documents. This allows public quiz retrieval queries to filter by these attributes (e.g., `grade=1`, `subject='Math'`) directly on the `quizQuestions` collection without needing to fetch data from `ContentChunk` or `SourceDocument` documents for rule evaluation.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **Structural Segregation:** The most significant strategy for QAPs is segregating administrative/content-creation data from public/student-facing data:
    *   **`/users/{userId}/sourceDocuments` and `/users/{userId}/sourceDocuments/{sourceDocumentId}/contentChunks`**: These collections are strictly user-owned and managed. Security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path variable) for all operations. Queries within these paths automatically respect the `userId`, making rules simple and preventing data leakage.
    *   **`/quizQuestions`**: This is a top-level collection designed for public consumption. Documents here are readable by any authenticated (or even anonymous, if rules permit) user, enabling efficient `list` operations (quizzes for students). The `ownerId` within each `QuizQuestion` document is used *only* for write/update/delete permissions, not for read access. This allows clients to query for quizzes by `topic`, `difficulty`, `grade`, and `subject` (all denormalized into `QuizQuestion`) without security rules needing to filter results, as read access is broadly granted.
    *   **`/students/{externalAuthId}` and `/students/{externalAuthId}/studentAnswers`**: These collections are designed for private, user-specific data. The `externalAuthId` in the path explicitly matches `request.auth.uid`, making authorization rules trivial (`request.auth.uid == externalAuthId`). Queries within these paths are inherently secure, adhering to QAPs.

**Other Principles:**
*   **Clarity of Intent**: The hierarchical path structure clearly delineates administrative content, publicly available quizzes, and student-specific performance data.
*   **DBAC**: Roles are implicitly managed through `ownerId` fields in documents and `externalAuthId` matching `request.auth.uid` in paths, avoiding custom claims.
*   **Invariants**: Denormalized fields (`ownerId`, `sourceDocumentId`, `grade`, `subject` in `ContentChunk` and `QuizQuestion`) support the integrity of ownership and content context, simplifying rule enforcement and ensuring data consistency.
